stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 15 patients. The sample mean is 381.3 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 123.9 mmHg. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 368.1 mmHg and standard deviation of 93.5 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 381.3, 95% CI = [312.7, 449.9]. Without outliers: mean = 368.1, 95% CI = [316.2, 420.0]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [312.7, 449.9]. Point estimate: 374.7 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [312.7, 449.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
